title: Kenyan-Style Yam Fritters with Sweet Stir-Fry Sauce
description: A French-inspired twist on traditional Kenyan cuisine, featuring crispy yam fritters served with a sweet and sticky stir-fry sauce.

ingredients.name: kenyan
ingredients.notes: Kenyan chili powder (or substitute with a blend of chili and garlic powder)

instructions: 1. In a large bowl, combine the flour, milk, and a pinch of salt. Mix well to form a smooth batter.
2. Add the diced yams to the bowl and stir until they are evenly coated with the batter.
3. Heat about 1-2 inches of oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
4. Using a spoon, drop small amounts of the yam batter into the hot oil, being careful not to overcrowd the pan.
5. Fry the yam fritters for about 3-4 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and crispy.
6. Remove the fritters from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
7. In a small saucepan, combine the honey, Kenyan chili powder, and a pinch of salt. Heat the mixture over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sauce is smooth and sticky.
8. To serve, place a few yam fritters on a plate and drizzle with the sweet stir-fry sauce.
9. Garnish with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
